description:
You are the system administrator preparing an isolated maintenance
    environment that cannot rely on the system-wide */etc/hosts* or on an
    external DNS server.  
    Your goal is to build a **self-contained name-resolution workspace
    under */home/user/dns_test***.

    Perform the following steps:

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/dns_test  

    2. Inside that directory create a text file called  
       /home/user/dns_test/hosts_custom  
       containing **exactly** the five lines shown below, **one blank line at
       the end** and **each field separated by a single space**:

       127.0.0.1 localhost  
       172.20.1.10 stage-db.internal db  
       172.20.1.11 corp-git.internal git  
       172.20.1.12 metrics.internal metrics  
       172.20.1.13 backup-srv.internal backup  
       ␤ ← *(this is the single mandatory trailing blank line)*

       • First column  = IPv4 address  
       • Remaining columns = one or more host-aliases for that IP

    3. Still in the same directory, create a query list called  
       /home/user/dns_test/host_query.list  
       with the **four** hostnames below, **one per line and in
       this exact order**:

       stage-db.internal  
       corp-git.internal  
       metrics.internal  
       backup-srv.internal  

    4. Write an executable Bash script  
       /home/user/dns_test/resolve_hosts.sh  
       that behaves as follows when run with no arguments:

       a) Reads */home/user/dns_test/host_query.list* line-by-line.  
       b) For each hostname, finds the first matching record in
          */home/user/dns_test/hosts_custom* and extracts the IPv4 address
          that precedes it.  
       c) Prints the mapping to STDOUT in the format  

          hostname -> ip  

          (example: `stage-db.internal -> 172.20.1.10`)  

       d) Writes **only** the CSV form of the mapping to the file  
          /home/user/dns_test/resolve.log, overwriting any previous content.
          The CSV form is  

          hostname,ip  

          (no spaces).  One mapping per line, same order as in
          *host_query.list*.  The file **must end with a newline**.

    5. Run your script once so that */home/user/dns_test/resolve.log* is
       populated.

    OUTPUT FORMATS THAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY CHECKED
    =================================================
    A) /home/user/dns_test/hosts_custom  
       • Exactly 5 non-blank lines plus one blank terminating line.  
       • Single spaces only, no tabs.

    B) /home/user/dns_test/host_query.list  
       • Exactly 4 lines, no trailing blank line.

    C) /home/user/dns_test/resolve.log  
       • Exactly the 4 CSV lines, ending with **one** newline.  
         Expected final appearance:

         stage-db.internal,172.20.1.10  
         corp-git.internal,172.20.1.11  
         metrics.internal,172.20.1.12  
         backup-srv.internal,172.20.1.13␤

    D) /home/user/dns_test/resolve_hosts.sh  
       • Must have its execute bit set (`chmod +x`).  
       • Running it a second time should overwrite *resolve.log* with the
         same correct content.

    The automated tests will:

    • Verify the presence and permissions of the directory and files.  
    • Inspect the precise byte-for-byte contents of
      *hosts_custom*, *host_query.list*, and *resolve.log*.  
    • Execute *resolve_hosts.sh* and compare its STDOUT
      and the regenerated *resolve.log* to the required formats.

    Do not modify any files outside */home/user/dns_test*.  
    Kernel-level or root privileges are NOT available, so rely solely on
    user-accessible commands and scripts.
truth:
Expected state after successful completion:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/dns_test (exists, default user permissions)

    2. File:
       /home/user/dns_test/hosts_custom  
       Contents (including the terminating blank line):
       -----------------------------
       127.0.0.1 localhost
       172.20.1.10 stage-db.internal db
       172.20.1.11 corp-git.internal git
       172.20.1.12 metrics.internal metrics
       172.20.1.13 backup-srv.internal backup

       -----------------------------

    3. File:
       /home/user/dns_test/host_query.list  
       Contents (no trailing blank line):
       -----------------------------
       stage-db.internal
       corp-git.internal
       metrics.internal
       backup-srv.internal
       -----------------------------

    4. File:
       /home/user/dns_test/resolve.log  
       Contents (exactly four lines, single trailing newline):
       -----------------------------
       stage-db.internal,172.20.1.10
       corp-git.internal,172.20.1.11
       metrics.internal,172.20.1.12
       backup-srv.internal,172.20.1.13
       -----------------------------

    5. Executable script:
       /home/user/dns_test/resolve_hosts.sh  
       • Executable bit set.  
       • When executed, STDOUT must be:
         stage-db.internal -> 172.20.1.10
         corp-git.internal -> 172.20.1.11
         metrics.internal -> 172.20.1.12
         backup-srv.internal -> 172.20.1.13
         (with newlines after each line, no extra blank line at
         the end).

    The test harness will:
       a) Check file paths and permissions.  
       b) Hash and compare file contents.  
       c) Run `bash /home/user/dns_test/resolve_hosts.sh` and verify
          both STDOUT and the regenerated *resolve.log* match the
          specified expectations above.
